query_normalized: statistical bias in pre-print server publication trends statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) by measuring a key dependent variable (changes in reference lists and literature framing) within the user's specific domain (preprint-publication pairs), providing empirical data on how scholarly communication evolves from preprint to publication. It also aligns with criterion (c) by establishing a baseline for reference stability (90% unchanged) that is essential for analyzing publication trends and potential biases in the preprint ecosystem.
